perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement. However, Section 6 can be verbally amended by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires written amendments. The added sentence allows verbal amendments to Section 6, creating a contradiction. The original clause claims the agreement can be amended by 'separate writing', while the added sentence contradicts this point, and allows verbal ammendments. It is unclear which terms should be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement. However, Section 6 can be verbally amended by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text are the same, and they both include a contradictory sentence. The first part of the clause requires all amendments to be in writing, while the added sentence permits verbal amendments to Section 6. This creates a direct conflict within the agreement as to how amendments can be made, leading to uncertainty and a strong contradiction, and a clear 'YES' is warranted.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party can keep a copy of any information related to marketing strategy.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that all copies of Confidential Information must be returned or destroyed. The addition, 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party can keep a copy of any information related to marketing strategy,' allows the Receiving Party to retain specific Confidential Information, contradicting the 'all copies' requirement. The original text specifies everything has to be destroyed, but the added text implies that the receiving party may keep some copies related to marketing strategy, making this an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information . Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party can keep a copy of any information related to marketing strategy.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that all confidential information must be returned or destroyed. The changed text adds an exception, allowing the receiving party to retain copies of information related to marketing strategy. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. This directly contradicts the 'all copies' requirement, causing an in-text contradiction. The changed text alters the original obligation of returning/destroying *all* confidential information by allowing an exception, making these requirements incompatible and therefore is a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that a party has breached this Agreement, then such party shall be liable and pay to the non-breaching party the legal fees and expenses such non-breaching party has incurred in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that a party has breached this Agreement, then such party shall be liable and pay to the non-breaching party the legal fees and expenses such non-breaching party has incurred in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom. However, the Receiving Party is not responsible for paying any legal fees if they were acting in good faith.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the breaching party to pay legal fees. The added sentence exempts the Receiving Party from paying if they acted in good faith, creating a contradiction regarding fee responsibility. The original text indicates that legal fees must be payed, while the modified text explains that the Receiving party doesn't have to pay.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that a party has breached this Agreement, then such party shall be liable and pay to the non-breaching party the legal fees and expenses such non-breaching party has incurred in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom. However, the Receiving Party is not responsible for paying any legal fees if they were acting in good faith.
perturbation.justification: The original clause states that the breaching party 'shall be liable and pay' legal fees. The modification adds an exception, stating the Receiving Party isn't responsible if acting in good faith. This directly contradicts the 'shall be liable and pay' obligation, creating an inconsistency regarding fee responsibility, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. The Changed text and Contradicted text are the same in this case, but the change to the original text introduced the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise specified herein, the obligations of the parties set forth in this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise specified herein, the obligations of the parties set forth in this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect eighteen months from the date hereof. However, the obligation to keep information confidential in section 1 will extend indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The original termination clause states the agreement ends after eighteen months. The addition, 'However, the obligation to keep information confidential in section 1 will extend indefinitely' contradicts this by extending a specific obligation (confidentiality) indefinitely beyond the eighteen-month term. The first part implies an agreement term of 18 months, while the added text extends this term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise specified herein, the obligations of the parties set forth in this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect eighteen months from the date hereof. However, the obligation to keep information confidential in section 1 will extend indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all obligations terminate after eighteen months. The changed text adds an exception, extending the confidentiality obligation indefinitely. This creates a direct conflict with the original termination clause, because it extends one specific obligation past the original termination date, thus creating an inconsistency. The Contradicted Text is in the same location as the changed text, therefore the change introduced a contradiction.
